decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to quickly assemble a board of artifact creatures and take advantage of powerful artifact synergies. By using Mox Opal and Urza's Saga to generate mana, along with Emry, Lurker of the Loch to recur artifacts from the graveyard, the deck can quickly overwhelm opponents with threats like Pinnacle Emissary and Patchwork Automaton. Additional disruption in the form of Sink into Stupor and Force of Will helps control the game, while cards like Chalice of the Void and Pithing Needle can disrupt opponent strategies. The deck aims to win through aggressive plays and powerful artifact interactions.

The deck focuses on assembling a combo using Doomsday to set up a win condition with either Thassa's Oracle or Jace, Wielder of Mysteries. It includes cards such as Lotus Petal, Dark Ritual, and Lion's Eye Diamond to generate mana quickly, as well as disruption spells like Force of Will and Teferi, Time Raveler to protect the combo. The deck also includes card selection tools like Ponder and Brainstorm to find key pieces of the combo. The goal is to efficiently combo off and win the game in a single turn.

The deck focuses on utilizing Show and Tell to put powerful creature cards, such as Emrakul, the Aeons Torn, into play for free. The deck also includes ways to protect this strategy with counterspells like Force of Will and protection spells like Veil of Summer. By setting up the combo with cards like Ponder and Brainstorm, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats. The inclusion of Cunning Wish allows for additional flexibility in finding the right answers to any situation, making it a versatile and potent combo deck.

The Boros Aggro deck aims to quickly overwhelm and outpace the opponent by utilizing efficient creatures and disruptive spells. With a focus on aggression and tempo, the deck's main strategy is to play threats early on and back them up with removal spells to control the board. Cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah, Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, and Swords to Plowshares help to establish an aggressive board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan. By applying pressure and maintaining board control, the Boros Aggro deck seeks to secure a swift victory before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on quickly comboing off with a high density of spells to mill the entire library, then win the game with Thassa's Oracle's "win the game" ability. By utilizing fast mana like Simian Spirit Guide, Dark Ritual, and Lotus Petal, along with tutors like Reanimate and Pact of Negation for protection, the deck aims to assemble the combo as quickly as possible. The deck also includes ways to disrupt the opponent's gameplan with cards like Cabal Therapy and Pact of Negation, while minimizing the downside of milling oneself with cards like Narcomoeba and Memory's Journey.

The deck focuses on using artifact cards to control the game and create a prison-like environment for the opponent. By utilizing cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Trinisphere, it aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and prevent them from effectively playing their cards. Additionally, the deck includes powerful artifacts like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn to further dominate the battlefield. Using the powerful synergy between artifacts and control elements, the deck aims to establish a dominant position and overwhelm the opponent.

The main focus of this Legacy 1 vs 1 Aggro - UR Tempo deck is to quickly apply pressure on the opponent while disrupting their game plan through cards like Lightning Bolt, Wasteland, and Force of Will. By using efficient threats like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent, along with cantrips such as Brainstorm and Ponder, the deck aims to maintain card advantage and tempo advantage. With a mix of removal spells like Unholy Heat and disruption like Daze, the deck seeks to control the pace of the game and secure victory with aggressive plays.

The main concept of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through a combination of control elements like Force of Will and Thoughtseize, while also applying pressure with efficient creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Murktide Regent. The deck utilizes card advantage with Brainstorm and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, while also utilizing removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out to maintain board presence. The deck aims to quickly close out the game by applying pressure and disrupting the opponent's plays, ultimately leading to victory through a combination of tempo and aggression.

The core idea of this deck is to maintain control over the game while simultaneously applying pressure to the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. By combining the tempo elements of Dimir colors with aggressive creatures like Murktide Regent and Nethergoyf, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while continuing to advance its own strategy. Utilizing versatile cards such as Thoughtseize, Force of Will, and Daze, the deck seeks to keep the opponent off balance and unable to effectively respond to its threats, ultimately leading to a decisive victory.

The deck focuses on locking down the opponent's plays by using a combination of artifacts to control the board. It aims to slow down the opponent's strategy with cards like Trinisphere and Pithing Needle, while also generating value from Urza's Saga and Karn, the Great Creator. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like The One Ring and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn can help close out the game quickly once the opponent's options have been limited. The deck's strategy revolves around creating a prison-like environment for the opponent to navigate through.

This deck focuses on aggressive play, utilizing cards that allow for quick spells and creatures to control the tempo of the game. With a strong mana base and various versatile spells like Lightning Bolt and Force of Will, this deck can quickly disrupt opponents' strategies while maintaining pressure with creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent. The inclusion of resilient creatures and disruptive spells ensures that this deck can adapt to and overcome various threats, granting it an advantage in fast-paced matches.

This deck focuses on using the graveyard as a resource to quickly bring powerful creatures back into play, bypassing their mana costs. It combines cards like Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Shallow Grave with creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty to establish a dominating board presence early in the game. Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy help disrupt the opponent's game plan while cards like Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers and Atraxa, Grand Unifier provide additional value and control elements. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast, explosive plays and efficient reanimation strategies.

The deck focuses on quickly controlling the game by disrupting opponent's plays with counter spells and hand disruption spells, while applying pressure with efficient creatures. It also aims to maintain tempo by utilizing cheap removal spells and land destruction. The deck includes powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Nethergoyf for closing out the game. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares provide additional card advantage and disruption options, while maintaining a low mana curve for aggressive plays. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while presenting a fast clock.

The focus of this combo deck is to quickly and efficiently mill through the entire deck, ending with Thassa's Oracle as the final piece to win the game. By using cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer, along with Dark Ritual and Cabal Ritual for mana acceleration, the deck aims to dump all spells into the graveyard to set up the win condition. The inclusion of Pact of Negation helps protect the combo, while cards like Reanimate and Dread Return provide ways to bring back key pieces if necessary. This deck is all about going all in on the combo and trying to win before the opponent has a chance to defend.

The deck focuses on using disruptive spells such as Thoughtseize and Smallpox to control the opponent's resources while generating advantage through cards like Life from the Loam and Urza's Saga. It aims to grind out the game by managing the opponent's board state and resources, ultimately overwhelming them with powerful threats like Hogaak and Grist. The deck also utilizes land destruction with cards like Wasteland and Ghost Quarter to further disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck is designed to control the game and outvalue the opponent through resource management and disruptive spells.
